questions.question: What is a constitution?
questions.correct_answer: A set of fundamental rules and principles according to which a country is governed
questions.explanation: A constitution defines the powers of government and the rights of citizens in a country.

questions.question: What was apartheid?
questions.correct_answer: A system of racial discrimination practiced in South Africa
questions.explanation: Under apartheid, non-white people were denied equal rights and treated unfairly.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the struggle against apartheid in South Africa?
questions.explanation: Nelson Mandela fought against racial discrimination and later became the first black President of South Africa.

questions.question: Why do countries need a constitution?
questions.correct_answer: To define government powers, protect citizen rights and establish rules for governance
questions.explanation: A constitution creates trust, limits government authority and guides democratic governance.

questions.question: What was the Constituent Assembly?
questions.correct_answer: A body of elected representatives that drafted the Constitution of India
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly debated and finalized the provisions of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Who was the chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar played a key role in drafting the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: When was the Constitution of India adopted?
questions.explanation: The Constitution was adopted in 1949 and came into effect on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: What is the Preamble?
questions.correct_answer: An introductory statement that explains the philosophy and values of the Constitution
questions.explanation: The Preamble highlights key ideals such as justice, liberty, equality and fraternity.

questions.question: What does the term 'Sovereign' mean in the Preamble?
questions.correct_answer: India has the power to make its own decisions independently
questions.explanation: Sovereignty means freedom from external control in internal and external matters.

questions.question: What does 'Democratic Republic' mean?
questions.correct_answer: A system where leaders are elected by the people and the head of state is not hereditary
questions.explanation: Citizens elect representatives and the government is accountable to them.

notes.title: Constitution Definition
notes.content: A constitution is the supreme law of a country that defines the structure of government and the rights of citizens.

notes.title: Apartheid System
notes.content: Apartheid was a policy of racial segregation in South Africa where non-white people were denied basic rights.

notes.title: Constituent Assembly
notes.content: The Constituent Assembly of India was formed in 1946 to draft the Constitution and included representatives from different regions and communities.

notes.title: The Preamble
notes.content: The Preamble introduces the values and guiding principles of the Constitution such as justice, liberty, equality and fraternity.

notes.title: Guiding Values
notes.content: The Constitution promotes democratic ideals including sovereignty, socialism, secularism and democracy.

facts.fact: Nelson Mandela spent 27 years in prison for opposing apartheid in South Africa.
The Indian Constitution is one of the longest written constitutions in the world.
The Preamble begins with the words 'We, the People of India'.
Republic Day in India is celebrated on 26 January to mark the day the Constitution came into effect.
